questions.question: Explain what NVIDIA compute capability is and how it is used in CUDA programming. Include how to query device properties.
keyPoints: Compute capability is a version number (major.minor) defining the hardware feature set of a GPU
It determines supported features, hardware limits, and instruction set support
cudaGetDeviceProperties() queries all properties at runtime including SM count, memory sizes, and max threads

questions.question: Discuss the evolution of NVIDIA GPU architectures from Fermi to Hopper. For each major generation, describe the key features introduced and how they impacted CUDA programming. Explain how to compile CUDA code for multiple architectures.
keyPoints: Fermi (CC 2.x): L1/L2 caches, ECC, concurrent kernels, 1024 threads per block
Kepler (CC 3.x): Dynamic parallelism, Hyper-Q, shuffle instructions
Pascal (CC 6.x): NVLink, HBM2, unified memory page migration, FP16 support
Volta/Turing (CC 7.x): Tensor Cores, independent thread scheduling, RT Cores
Ampere/Hopper (CC 8.x/9.0): TF32, structured sparsity, async copy, Thread Block Clusters, Transformer Engine
